creator: jaretbartsch@yahoo.ca
text:
---704894761-19936695-1362180926=:58847 Content-Type: text/plain; charset=us-ascii As suggested to do so, I am making a followup with the following as extra information to the initial filing of this PR: The problem is that when you want to use a port for one or the other, it says it conflicts because the other horde port is installed. eg: imp will work with horde-webmail, but won't for horde-groupware when horde groupware does / should allow use of the imp port. this problem also follows with pear ports too so either you're in with one port or you're out and can't use it when you should be able to with the other. if you uninstall the ports (pear, etc) that are used for one or the other that the other doesn't allow everything works peachy, but it's a sacrifice one or the other for the other scenario and really does limit the functionality of horde-groupware. Personally I would recommend just to keep horde-groupware and go remove horde-webmail because horde-groupware does everything horde-webmail does and more. At least it seems more practical / logical to me. This would also make maintaining the project much simpler with less work to maintain two separate ports of the same project, but that's just my opinion. ---704894761-19936695-1362180926=:58847 Content-Type: text/html; charset=us-ascii
As suggested to do so, I am making a followup with the following as extra information to the initial filing of this PR:
